title: Pithecellobium latifolium
taxonomy: Pithecellobium latifolium is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant belongs to the genus Pithecellobium, which is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. Within the genus, Pithecellobium latifolium is classified as a species of tree or shrub. It is also known by other names, such as the "Guamachil" or "Guamuchil".
benefits: Pithecellobium latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved entrance tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds and skin irritations. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in fighting against certain types of bacteria and fungi. In traditional medicine, Pithecellobium latifolium is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's potential health benefits and to fully understand its effects on human health.
constituents: Pithecellobium latifolium is a plant species found in various regions, but specific information on its active constituents is not readily available. parts: Pithecellobium latifolium is a plant species native to tropical regions. The leaves and bark of Pithecellobium latifolium are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
